{{Quote|The Twi'leki designation for them is Chir'daki. In your Basic it would be Deathseed. It recalls the spores of a parasitic fungus that invades a larger creature and destroys it. Most unpleasant, as would be facing our Chir'daki in combat.|Tal'dira to Wedge Antilles|<ref name="X-Wing: The Bacta War">''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Chir'daki''''', known in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] as '''Deathseed''', was a unique type of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] developed and used by the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] species.

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|I thought the fighters you have created were impressive. Their maneuverability, I would imagine, makes them very formidable.|Wedge Antilles to Tal'dira|<ref name="X-Wing: The Bacta War" />}}
The fighter was actually an "[[Ugly/Legends|Ugly]]" design, having been assembled from parts of both the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] and the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]. Unlike typical Uglies, which were haphazardly cobbled together from whatever parts were available, the ''Chir'daki'' was built according to a specific design and apparently had no more variation from one fighter to the next than would a factory-built craft.

The [[S-foils/Legends|S-foils]] of the X-wing fighter were attached to a gyroscopic mount which was then mounted onto the main pod of a [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] fighter, similar to the design of the [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]]. The Death Seed used the twin [[Ion engine/Legends|ion engines]] of the TIE for propulsion, and relied on the X-wing engines to power the [[Laser cannon/Legends|lasers]], [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]. The resulting craft had a bit better maneuverability than the standard TIE, but was a little slower. The Chir'daki starfighters were also related to TIEs in the fact that they also had no life support systems, hence their pilots had to wear pressurized flight suits like Imperial pilots did.

The S-foils gave the Death Seed all the laser power of an X-wing, giving it more firepower than a TIE/LN. The Twi'leks decided not to incorporate any [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] or [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launchers into the design, as those weapons were very difficult to acquire.

==History==
A squadron of these fighters led by [[Tal'dira]] joined [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and the former members of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] during the [[Bacta War]].
